meta.title: PWG->RU research-capability observatory
meta.confidence_meaning: Confidence in the STATUS LABEL, not in the layer: high = grounded against committed code/data on disk this pass; medium = based on the 2026-07-09 ground-truth notes; low = roadmap framing only, not independently re-verified.
meta.grounding_note: code_inputs are committed scripts, so the generator's on-disk probe is a real signal. data_inputs are frequently gitignored bulk (corpus_lexicon.jsonl, *_translated.jsonl), so 'absent in tree' does NOT mean the dataset is missing; those are declared-only.

cards.title: COMET-QE calibration for A/B/C translation grades
cards.claim: Reference-free QE scores correlate with human A/B/C grades strongly enough to replace or reweight the hand proxy in tm_grade.py.
cards.coverage_note: tm_grade.py proxy exists; no COMET run, no frozen human A/B/C gold slice adjudicated yet.
cards.blocker: Needs a frozen adjudicated A/B/C gold slice + COMET Sa->Ru transfer check.

cards.title: Awesome-align/SimAlign agreement as a word-alignment confidence gate
cards.claim: Word pairs where an independent aligner agrees with the DeepSeek alignment have higher precision than disagreements.
cards.coverage_note: tm_align.py produces the DeepSeek alignment; no second independent aligner wired, no agreement-bucket precision computed.
cards.blocker: Install/run awesome-align or SimAlign, bucket by agreement, measure precision.

cards.title: BLI evaluation of corpus_lexicon with P@1/MRR
cards.claim: The 1.09M-row Sa->Ru alignment lexicon performs above a most-frequent-translation baseline on bilingual lexicon induction.
cards.coverage_note: corpus_lexicon has coverage numbers but no BLI-style P@1/P@5/MRR evaluation against a gold set.
cards.blocker: Draw a 300-item dictionary-backed Sa->Ru gold set; compute P@1/P@5/MRR + baseline delta.

cards.title: Token-in-context PWG sense disambiguation for DCS passages
cards.claim: A DCS token's PWG sense is predictable from local context above a most-frequent-sense baseline.
cards.coverage_note: Genuinely new (WSD absent). Needs DCS token contexts (VisualDCS) + a translated PWG sense inventory + gold sense labels.
cards.blocker: Requires card 5 (MFS baseline) first, plus gold sense labels for high-frequency polysemous lemmas.

cards.title: Most-frequent-sense baseline per lemma
cards.claim: A simple MFS baseline is necessary to keep WSD and learner-ranking claims honest.
cards.coverage_note: Deterministic: group store rows by key1, join DCS frequency, emit an MFS candidate + explicit 'unknown' when evidence absent. Highest downstream leverage (unblocks 4, 8, 11).
cards.blocker: None (local-only). First honesty floor.

cards.title: Multi-translator consensus score for grade-A TM candidates
cards.claim: Cross-translator agreement predicts safer grade-A TM rows.
cards.coverage_note: Multi-reference verse groups exist in corpus_lexicon; consensus rate vs adjudicated grade not computed. Rights-gated only for release, not for the internal score.
cards.blocker: Compute agreement by key/segment; compare grade distribution consensus vs singleton.

cards.title: Variant-reading extractor from systematic translator divergence
cards.claim: Repeated disagreement among reputable translators identifies genuine sense variation rather than mere noise.
cards.coverage_note: Needs >=50 reviewed divergence clusters classified at kappa>=0.60 and mapped to PWG senses vs 'style' buckets.
cards.blocker: Gold review of divergence clusters + rights clearance on translator sources.

cards.title: Per-sense confidence score
cards.claim: A composite confidence score over evidence, genre, government, and TM agreement predicts review priority better than any single signal.
cards.coverage_note: Buildable deterministically from in-store evidence[]/government/genre/TM grades; final calibration (AUC vs failures) needs a frozen validation slice.
cards.blocker: Deterministic to build; AUC validation needs gold. government + evidence fields are currently 0/11,261 populated (see cards 23, 9).

cards.title: Contradiction lane for sources that actively disagree
cards.claim: Distinguishing contradiction from silence improves evidence readability and catches risky senses.
cards.coverage_note: annotate_evidence.py assembles evidence but 0/11,261 rows carry per-source supports/contradicts/silent markers today; the 'contradicts' lane is not emitted.
cards.blocker: Retrofit per-source relation vector offline (no re-translation), then spot-check 'contradicts' precision on 50 items.

cards.title: Per-sense diachronic first/last attestation band
cards.claim: PWG senses can be dated more informatively as first/last citation bands, not only as coarse Renou states.
cards.coverage_note: sense_stratum.py + pwg_sense_stratum.jsonl (~23,461 senses) already give a Renou-state proxy per sense. Gap: absolute date_min/date_max bands from per-sense <ls> citations via ls_source_map.json.
cards.blocker: Join per-sense <ls> to ls_source_map dates; count unmapped citations separately.

cards.title: Lexical semantic-change detector over period/genre strata
cards.claim: Sense distribution across periods reveals measurable semantic change or register narrowing.
cards.coverage_note: Depends on card 10 date bands + DCS genre/frequency dims; top-100 shifted lemmas need >=70% meaningful on review.
cards.blocker: Needs card 10 shipped and a review pass over top-shifted lemmas.

cards.title: "Still alive" score for senses found in live/corpus usage
cards.claim: DCS and other corpus evidence can rank PWG senses by likely current or corpus-attested usefulness.
cards.coverage_note: DCS attestation + per-sense evidence give a computable score; 'unknown' must stay explicit for senses with no corpus route.
cards.blocker: Reviewer agreement that high-score senses are more useful for readers.

cards.title: Dual-model mined-pair acceptance score
cards.claim: Two independent model judgments reduce mined-pair false positives at an acceptable recall cost.
cards.coverage_note: 10,132 mined pairs + existing precision samples exist; second-model verdicts + precision/recall-by-policy not run.
cards.blocker: Second-model pass + policy calibration to publication-grade precision, else document human-adjudication fallback.

cards.title: Bicleaner-style bitext filter for mined Sa/Ru pairs
cards.claim: A learned or feature-based bitext filter generalizes better than hard-coded thresholds for mined pairs.
cards.coverage_note: Needs ROC-AUC / precision@k improvement over the current threshold gate without hiding proper-name/list-scope failures.
cards.blocker: Labelled accepted/rejected examples to train/evaluate the filter.

cards.title: Segment-level provenance for every TMX row
cards.claim: Every exported TMX row can explain its source, method, grade, rights state, and model involvement.
cards.coverage_note: build_tmx.py + tm_grade.py already emit source/method/grade fields; the deterministic gap is a validated provenance schema with 100% coverage and rights-gated exclusion.
cards.blocker: Define the provenance schema, validate 100% of exportable rows, mark/exclude rights-gated rows.

cards.title: Independent bilingual-lexicon drift detector
cards.claim: A model-independent bilingual lexicon can flag alignment drift and hallucinated Sa->Ru pairs.
cards.coverage_note: Flagged disagreements must have >=50% actionable value or the detector is deferred as too noisy.
cards.blocker: Needs external BLI tooling/embeddings + reviewed flag sample.

cards.title: Bad-reuse detector for stale/rejected fragment TM reuse
cards.claim: Fragment TM reuse can be audited for stale, rejected, or superseded content before it reaches new cards.
cards.coverage_note: Fragment TM sidecars, denylist, and window_selftest.py fixtures exist; the detector (catch seeded stale/rejected, zero FP on known-good) is not built.
cards.blocker: None (local-only). Build detector against the selftest fixtures.

cards.title: LaBSE margin-mining path for unanchored prose
cards.claim: Embedding margin mining recovers useful Sa/Ru pairs from prose texts where verse anchors are unavailable.
cards.coverage_note: precision@100 must justify a larger Track-A prose ingestion run, else prose stays blocked on manual anchoring.
cards.blocker: Rights-clear Sa/Ru prose pilots (Leitan-style) + LaBSE mining.

cards.title: Vecalign-style long-document prose alignment
cards.claim: Long-document alignment improves continuity and recall over independent segment mining for prose translations.
cards.coverage_note: Vecalign or equivalent must beat plain nearest-neighbor mining on a pilot text.
cards.blocker: Same prose candidates as card 18 + paragraph boundaries.

cards.title: Rights-aware ingestion queue for candidate source texts
cards.claim: Source acquisition can be managed as a structured queue so research candidates do not silently become publishable data.
cards.coverage_note: Deterministic bookkeeping: every external-data capability must point to a queue item with a rights state. Gates cards 18/19/21/22.
cards.blocker: None technical; a governance/registry artifact (public-domain/permissioned/private-use/blocked/unknown).

cards.title: Parallel-passage finder by PWG lemma
cards.claim: For a queried PWG lemma, the system can surface aligned Sa/Ru passages that instantiate the sense evidence.
cards.coverage_note: corpus_lexicon + SamudraManthanam corpus.db + PWG store give retrieval material; top-5 passages must be useful in >=80% of a reviewed sample.
cards.blocker: Retrieval index + reviewed precision@k.

cards.title: Sanskrit compound segmentation lane before alignment/mining
cards.claim: Sanskrit segmentation before alignment improves recall for compounds and derived forms.
cards.coverage_note: Compound recall must improve without exceeding an agreed false-segmentation ceiling on a reviewed sample.
cards.blocker: sanskrit-util normalization + optional Samsaadhanii/Heritage validation + gold.

cards.title: Morphosyntactic compatibility check (case-government)
cards.claim: Sanskrit case/government evidence can catch incompatible Russian renderings or missing government notes.
cards.coverage_note: The sense schema already has a government field, but 0/11,261 rows populate it. The German marker is deterministic ((loc.), (loc. und gen.)) so a regex census + parser can backfill without an LLM. >=85% of high-severity mismatch flags must be real.
cards.blocker: None to build the census/backfill (local-only); mismatch-flag precision needs a review sample.

cards.title: Proper-name/transliteration classifier
cards.claim: A classifier separating names/transliterations from lexical translations reduces false mined-pair promotions.
cards.coverage_note: Must catch the known proper-name failure class at >=90% recall on a labelled sample.
cards.blocker: Labelled name/transliteration vs lexical sample.

cards.title: Inter-annotator agreement dashboard for gold slices
cards.claim: Every human-labelled evaluation slice should report agreement before it is used as ground truth.
cards.coverage_note: /gold-adjudicate already sets up multi-annotator review; the dashboard (kappa/alpha + label distribution, blocks 'gold' status when agreement missing) is not built.
cards.blocker: Wire review-sheet decisions + gold JSONL into an agreement computation.

cards.title: Data Statement / Datasheet generator for releases
cards.claim: Release artifacts can emit a consistent data statement from their manifests and provenance fields.
cards.coverage_note: DATASHEET.ru.md + release manifests + provenance fields exist; a generator covering knowable Bender/Friedman fields with explicit 'unknown' is not built.
cards.blocker: None (local-only); depends on card 15 provenance fields for full coverage.

cards.title: OntoLex/PROV-O upgrade for sense/evidence provenance
cards.claim: PWG->RU sense, evidence, genre, government, and provenance fields can be exported as a queryable LOD graph without losing store information.
cards.coverage_note: GROUNDED: release/ontolex.ttl + release/tei_lex0.xml + src/export_lod.py + src/export_interop.py exist (flat one-way export). Real gap: placeholder IRIs (example.org), no vartrans sense-links, no PROV-O, no LiLa link, no SPARQL. Reframe as UPGRADE, not stand-up.
cards.blocker: Real IRIs, vartrans/PROV-O modelling, SHACL profile alignment with csl-standards.

cards.title: SPARQL-style query layer over senses, genre, evidence, government
cards.claim: Research questions such as "all kavya senses supported by Kossovich with locative government" can be answered over the exported graph or a local query facade.
cards.coverage_note: At least 10 named research queries must return reproducible results with documented unknown/missing-data semantics. Depends on cards 23 (government), 9 (evidence relations), 27 (graph).
cards.blocker: Needs 23/9/27 populated; then a query facade or SPARQL endpoint.
data_inputs: LOD graph or store-derived query index

cards.title: Capability observatory
cards.claim: A single observatory can show which research layers exist, their coverage, confidence, blockers, and owner boundaries.
cards.coverage_note: THIS ARTIFACT. capability_observatory.json (registry) + build_observatory.py (generator) + CAPABILITY_OBSERVATORY.md + capability_observatory.html. All 30 cards carry status + dependency flag. Becomes 'shipped' once every status is re-verified against disk rather than seeded from notes.
cards.blocker: Per-card status re-verification pass (many are notes-seeded at medium/low confidence).

cards.title: Research-paper matrix
cards.claim: The roadmap can be turned into a paper agenda where every experiment has a claim, ACL precedent, dataset, metric, and expected figure.
cards.coverage_note: At least three coherent paper packages must emerge: one TM/QE paper, one sense/genre/WSD paper, one LOD/provenance data paper.
cards.blocker: None (local-only); best after the eval spine (1,3,5,25) produces measurable rows.
